summary: Crisp soundboard capture of an electrifying Euro‑'72 set, with a legendary Good Lovin', soaring Other One and tight Jack Straw leading the way.
review: The recording delivers a clean, well‑balanced soundboard mix (with a little hiss noted by some) that lets the band’s 1972 European energy shine through.  Highlights include an unforgettable Good Lovin’, a jaw‑dropping The Other One, and superb renditions of Jack Straw, China > Rider and Lovelight, while Jerry Garcia’s lead work and Phil Lesh’s melodic bass are repeatedly praised.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews (over 20) award five stars and praise both the performance and the mix; only minor complaints about hiss are noted, giving a strong combined show‑and‑recording score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with excellent instrument separation; several reviewers mention a faint hiss but overall consider it a high‑quality soundboard capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show builds nicely, with a solid first set and an especially powerful second‑set jam suite; reviewers note the second set as the highlight of the performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as “shredding”, “weaving magic” and “totally Jerry” – his lead work on The Other One and Good Lovin' is repeatedly praised.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh gets credit for melodic themes, especially during The Other One where he “starts up a great theme” and leads the transition into China > Rider.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is noted for rocking hard on Sugar Magnolia alongside Garcia.
